hero.subtitle: A practical China travel toolkit for phrases, payments, transport, food, and emergency help — all in one place.
hero.trust: Works inside China · No VPN needed · Free to start
hero.homeSubtitle: Your AI-powered travel companion for a smoother, safer, and more rewarding experience in China.
hero.mobileTrust: Trusted by travelers · 24/7 support · Your safety, our priority
hero.realMoments: Built for real moments in China — taxis, food, payments, hotels, and emergencies.
hero.mobileSubtitle: AI travel help for China, beautifully simplified.
hero.mobileTrustShort: No app · Mobile friendly · Free to start

before.title: Pre-Trip Checklist

items.visaSub: Required for most nationalities · Allow 2–4 weeks
items.vpnSub: Google, WhatsApp & most apps are blocked in China
items.wechat: Link a foreign card to WeChat Pay
items.mapsSub: Amap (高德) or Baidu Maps work without data
items.embassySub: In case of passport loss or emergency
items.paySetup: Set up Alipay or WeChat Pay
items.paySetupSub: Link a card or prepare another way to pay
items.hotelAddress: Keep your hotel address in Chinese
items.hotelAddressSub: Screenshot it for taxi drivers and locals
items.offlinePhrasesSub: Save key cards before you lose signal

sim.title: SIM Card
sim.body: Buy a China Unicom Tourist SIM at major airports — includes data & calls from Day 1.

apps.title: Apps to Install
apps.body: WeChat, Didi, Alipay, Meituan, Baidu Translate — install before you board.

cash.title: Cash or Card?
cash.body: Carry ¥500–1000 in cash for small vendors. Most malls accept Visa contactless.

before.cityGuidesSub: Airport, transport, payment, and local tips for each city.
before.cityUnlockToast: Unlock all 9 city survival guides with Trip Pass.

items.name: Alipay
items.purpose: For payments, taxis, metro, shops, and restaurants.
items.tip: Try setting it up before arrival.

items.name: WeChat
items.purpose: Messaging, payments, mini programs, and local contacts.
items.tip: Useful for daily communication in China.

items.name: Amap
items.purpose: Useful for local navigation in China.

items.name: Didi
items.purpose: Ride-hailing for taxis and private cars.
items.tip: Useful after landing or with luggage.

items.name: Trip.com
items.purpose: Useful for trains, flights, and hotels.
items.tip: Keep your passport name consistent when booking.

items: Set up Alipay or WeChat Pay before arrival if possible.
Keep a small amount of RMB cash as backup.
Foreign cards work mainly in hotels, malls, and larger stores.
Payment can fail sometimes; keep a phrase card ready.
Use Didi or official taxi queues when arriving with luggage.
Amap can be useful for local navigation in China.
High-speed trains are booked with your passport name.
Keep your hotel address in Chinese for drivers and station staff.

stay.phrasesSub: Tap any card to copy the Chinese text.

registration.title: Registration Required
registration.body: Hotels must register foreign guests with local police. Always carry your passport during check-in.

wifi.title: Hotel WiFi & VPN
wifi.body: Connect your VPN before using hotel WiFi. Chinese internet blocks most Western apps and sites.

address.title: Save Hotel Address
address.body: Screenshot your hotel address in Chinese characters — show it to taxi drivers directly.

stay.addressSub: Print your hotel address in Chinese for taxi rides

customHelper.title: Custom Phrase Helper
customHelper.subtitle: Create a phrase when hotel needs get specific.
customHelper.body: Create custom Chinese cards for your real travel situations.
customHelper.placeholder: What do you want to say?
customHelper.upgradeToast: Unlock custom phrase cards with Group Pass.

cityGuide.title: City Guide
cityGuide.subtitle: Pick your city for arrival, transport, payment, food, and safety basics.
cityGuide.upgradeToast: Unlock city survival packs with Trip Pass.

food.decoderSub: Upload or snap a photo of any Chinese menu — get instant translations with descriptions.
food.dropZoneSub: or tap to browse · JPG, PNG, HEIC

allergyCards.name: Peanut Allergy
allergyCards.phrase: I am allergic to peanuts. Please do not use peanuts or peanut oil in my food.
allergyCards.chinesePhrase: 我对花生过敏，请不要在我的食物中使用花生或花生油。

allergyCards.name: Shellfish Allergy
allergyCards.phrase: I am allergic to shellfish (shrimp, crab, lobster). Please keep these away from my dish.
allergyCards.chinesePhrase: 我对贝类过敏（虾、蟹、龙虾），请在我的菜肴中完全避免。

allergyCards.name: Gluten Free
allergyCards.phrase: I cannot eat gluten. Please avoid wheat, barley, and rye in my food.
allergyCards.chinesePhrase: 我不能食用麸质，请避免在我的食物中使用小麦、大麦和黑麦。

food.dishSearchPlaceholder: Search dish by name, Chinese, or pinyin…

transport.trainSub: High-speed rail (高铁) connects major cities. Book on Trip.com with your passport number.
transport.metroTrainPhrasesSub: Quick phrases for stations and transfers.

emergency.bannerSub: Tap any number to call directly. These work from any phone in China, even without a local SIM.
emergency.locationSub: Send your GPS coordinates to emergency services or your hotel concierge in Chinese.
emergency.embassyPhrase: Please contact my embassy. I am a foreign national and need consular assistance.
emergency.medicalPhrase: Please call an ambulance. I need urgent medical help.

numbers.policeDesc: Theft, assault, lost property, safety threats

emergency.lostPhonePhrase: I lost my phone. Please help me contact the police or my hotel.
emergency.copyFailed: Could not copy. Please copy the message manually.
emergency.locationCopied: Location copied in English and Chinese.
emergency.locationBlocked: Location permission was blocked. A backup message was copied.
emergency.locationUnavailable: Location is unavailable. A backup message was copied.

pay.refund: One-time payment · No auto-renewal · 3-day refund policy
pay.exchangeBody: Use bank ATMs (ICBC, Bank of China) for the best RMB exchange rates. Avoid airport kiosks and hotel desks.
pay.currencyLive: Live USD · EUR · GBP · AUD → RMB
pay.paymentSoon: Paid passes use manual PayPal payment links. Access is activated manually after payment.

free.name: Free
features: Apps, payments, taxis, food, hotels, and emergency help

trip.name: Trip Pass
trip.desc: Extra travel help for one traveler during a China trip
features: Extra travel support for one traveler

group.name: Group Pass
group.desc: Extra travel help for couples, families, or small groups visiting China together
features: Extra travel support for small groups

methods.name: WeChat Pay
methods.label: 微信支付
methods.desc: Link a foreign Visa/Mastercard. Accepted nearly everywhere.

methods.name: Alipay
methods.label: 支付宝
methods.desc: International version supports foreign cards. Great for online shopping.

methods.name: Visa / Mastercard
methods.label: 信用卡
methods.desc: Works at major hotels, malls, and high-end restaurants. Not widely accepted at local vendors.

methods.name: Cash (RMB)
methods.label: 人民币
methods.desc: Always carry ¥500 as a backup. Change foreign currency at ICBC or Bank of China.

setup.step3: Bind your foreign Visa or Mastercard
setup.step4: Set daily limit to match your travel budget
setup.step5: Enable fingerprint or Face ID for payments

tools.mobilePayBody: Most daily payments use QR codes.
tools.foreignCardsBody: Useful in hotels and larger stores, limited elsewhere.
tools.cashBody: Keep some RMB as a backup.
tools.failedBody: Show a short Chinese phrase when checkout gets stuck.

wechat.en: Can I pay with WeChat Pay?
wechat.note: Use when Alipay is not accepted.

cash.en: My card is not working. Can I pay in cash?
cash.note: Useful when a foreign card fails.

qr.en: Can you help me scan this QR code?
qr.note: Use when the QR flow is confusing.

chat.title: Ask Buddy
chat.subtitle: AI Travel Companion · Always on

suggestions.s1: How do I get from Shanghai airport to city center?
suggestions.s2: What should I do if I lose my passport?
suggestions.s3: How do I order food without speaking Chinese?
suggestions.s4: Is it safe to drink tap water?

chat.welcome: Hi! I'm Buddy, your AI travel companion for China. Ask me anything about getting around, eating safely, payments, or emergencies.
chat.quotaExceeded: You have used all {{limit}} Buddy AI messages on your {{plan}} plan. Upgrade to Trip Pass ($9.90) for 50 Buddy AI messages, or Group Pass ($29.90) for 200.
chat.notLoggedIn: Please sign in to use AI chat.
chat.trouble: I had trouble with that. Please try again.
chat.connectionIssue: Connection issue. Please check your internet and try again.
chat.planExpired: Your pass has expired. Please upgrade to continue using Buddy AI.
chat.dailyQuotaExceeded: You have reached today's limit of {{limit}} Buddy AI messages. Please try again tomorrow.
chat.needBuddy: Can't find what you need? Ask Buddy.
chat.verifyEmail: Please verify your email to use Buddy AI.
chat.verifyEmailHint: Check your inbox and refresh after verification.

auth.title: ChinaEase Buddy
auth.subtitle: Your account is saved securely with Firebase
auth.freeNote: Free plan includes 5 Buddy AI messages and 3 menu/photo scans. No credit card needed.
auth.passwordResetSent: Password reset email sent. Please check your inbox.
auth.passwordResetError: Could not send password reset email. Please check the email and try again.
auth.emailInUse: This email already has an account. Please log in instead.
auth.googlePopupClosed: Google sign-in was closed before it finished.
auth.googlePopupBlocked: Your browser blocked the Google sign-in popup. Please allow popups and try again.
auth.googleError: Could not sign in with Google. Please try again.
auth.verifyBeforeBuddy: Please verify your email before using Buddy AI.
auth.inAppBrowserNotice: Google sign-in may not work inside this app browser. Please open ChinaEase Buddy in Safari or Chrome to continue.
auth.inAppBrowserNoticeZh: 当前 App 内置浏览器可能无法使用 Google 登录。请用 Safari 或 Chrome 打开 chinaeasebuddy.com 后继续。
auth.emailAlternative: You can also sign in with email.

footer.tagline: AI travel companion for foreign visitors. Here to make your China journey easier, safer, and more rewarding.
footer.legalBody: ChinaEase Buddy provides general travel information and AI-assisted translations for convenience only. It is not a substitute for professional legal, medical, or financial advice. Emergency numbers and phrases are provided as reference — always verify with local authorities. ChinaEase does not guarantee the accuracy of translations, payment processing, or third-party service availability. Users are responsible for ensuring compliance with local laws and regulations.
footer.refundBody: Full refund within 3 days if no Buddy AI messages were used. Partial refund calculated by unused quota. Contact support to request.
footer.cookiesBody: We use essential cookies only: auth session and language preference. No advertising cookies.
footer.downloadInstructions: Open this site in your mobile browser and tap Share → Add to Home Screen

tabHeaders.before: Set up the essentials before landing in China.
tabHeaders.stay: Handle hotel check-in, room issues, and front desk conversations.
tabHeaders.food: Decode menus, avoid allergens, and order with confidence.
tabHeaders.transport: Taxi, metro, train, and airport help.
tabHeaders.pay: Understand Alipay, WeChat Pay, cards, and cash.

citySurvival.title: {{city}} Survival Pack
citySurvival.summary: Essential local tips for arriving, getting around, paying, eating, and staying safe.

airport: Use official airport transport or Didi.
Keep your hotel address in Chinese.
transport: Metro is usually reliable and low cost.
Didi is useful when you have luggage or late arrivals.
Amap can be useful for local navigation in China.
payment: Set up Alipay or WeChat Pay before arrival.
Keep some RMB cash as a backup.
Foreign cards are mainly useful in hotels and larger malls.
food: Try local signature dishes in small portions first.
Check spice level and allergens before ordering.
Use menu photos or phrase cards when staff do not speak English.
watch: Avoid unofficial taxis and unclear prices.
Be careful with invitations that lead to high bills.
Confirm prices before services or tours.
phrases: Please take me to this address.
Can I pay with Alipay or WeChat Pay?
Please help me call my hotel.

airport: Pudong Airport: use Maglev plus metro, or book Didi with your hotel address.
Hongqiao is useful for trains and domestic flights.
Avoid drivers who approach you inside the terminal.
transport: Metro is efficient for most city trips.
Didi is helpful after late meals or with luggage.
Keep the hotel address in Chinese for drivers.
payment: Alipay is widely used; set it up before arrival.
WeChat Pay is useful if your card links successfully.
Carry small RMB cash for backup at small vendors.
food: Try Xiaolongbao and Shengjianbao in small portions first.
Ask about crab, pork, peanuts, and spice if you have allergies.
Use menu photos when local restaurants have no English menu.
watch: Be careful with high-bill tea ceremony invitations.
Avoid non-metered taxis around tourist spots.
Check prices before buying souvenirs near attractions.
phrases: Please take me to this address.
Please help me call my hotel.
